fields.design_impact: This belongs partly in prompt-source wording and partly in validation and runtime policy. It reduces unsupported narrative fill and makes operator-facing answers more evidentiary and refusal-capable when grounding is weak.
fields.consult_when: Consult when updating grounded-answer wording, citation discipline, 'only say what actually happened' behavior, or fallback behavior when evidence is weak or absent.
fields.finding_summary: For grounded operator answers, explicitly constrain the model to supplied evidence, prefer exact contextual wording when appropriate, enable citations, and use Vertex AI Search or Answer API controls that skip adversarial, jailbreak, non-answer-seeking, or low-relevance queries. For stricter acceptance, use claim-support or grounding verification before treating the answer as final.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.2 official Google research: contextual prompting guidance, preamble guidance, AnswerGenerationSpec controls, Google Search grounding docs, and Check Grounding docs all support evidence-only answering, citations, ignore-flags, and claim-support verification.

fields.design_impact: This finding belongs directly in prompt-source files: require source-scoped, verifiable claims and forbid filling gaps with hidden-integration assumptions or invented retrieval.
fields.consult_when: Consult before editing prime or sub-agent prompt-source files for unknown, unavailable, or unconfigured enterprise-integration behavior.
fields.finding_summary: Google-documented preamble guidance supports instructing the app to keep claims verifiable within the given context and to suggest next steps or additional resources when grounded context is insufficient.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.4 bounded deep-research report in the 2026-04-22 session, based on official Google documentation for Gemini Enterprise preamble or custom-instruction guidance.

fields.design_impact: Search-truthfulness defects must distinguish platform indexing limits from query-generation defects.
fields.consult_when: Consult when investigating search misses or deciding whether a fallback query should broaden versus re-check source/indexing limits.
fields.finding_summary: Connector and indexing limits can independently cause misses, including searchable-text extraction limits and file-size/indexing limits, so retrieval failures are not safely attributable to planner quality alone.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 retrieval miss section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Future Gemini output-contract work should assume stronger schema control only on the Vertex/API side unless later research proves otherwise.
fields.consult_when: Consult before promising schema-locked output from the enterprise web surface or building DCOIR output validation around web-app structured-output assumptions.
fields.finding_summary: Structured output is explicitly documented on the Vertex model/API side, but the report did not find an equivalent first-class structured-output contract for Gemini Enterprise web-app or Agent Designer agents.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 7-9 and source appendix page 16. Vertex structured output support was documented while equivalent Gemini Enterprise web-app support remained undocumented.

fields.design_impact: DCOIR should prefer Agent Engine plus downstream system logs for proving what actually executed.
fields.consult_when: Consult before deciding where to anchor execution proof or analyst-grade audit evidence.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Engine provides the stronger runtime evidence plane for surfaced ADK agents through sessions, events, function-call traces, logging, monitoring, and downstream resource logs that can show both agent and user principals in delegated flows.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report observability/evidence sections.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini design must treat Model Armor coverage as a code-path responsibility for ADK agents, not a free inheritance from enterprise registration.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming enterprise console safety settings cover a custom runtime, or before describing the safety posture of registered ADK agents.
fields.finding_summary: Registered ADK agents in Gemini Enterprise remain an integration boundary. Gemini Enterprise can expose them, but Agent Engine processes the queries and Gemini Enterprise console Model Armor does not automatically protect the registered ADK agent path.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report page 7 and source appendix page 13. Report explicitly calls out the Model Armor caveat for registered ADK agents.

fields.design_impact: Move exact structure out of prose-only instructions and into schema-native constraints where the runtime path supports it. Keep prompt-source files focused on human-level behavior rules instead of maintaining a second schema copy that can cause duplicate headings or malformed sections.
fields.consult_when: Consult when updating operator-facing output contracts, final-form section order, or any Gemini path that must emit one stable machine-shaped or strongly structured answer.
fields.finding_summary: For strict final-form output, put the contract in responseSchema with responseMimeType JSON, required fields, and propertyOrdering. Do not duplicate the same schema in prompt prose because Google documents that duplicated schema text can degrade output quality or confuse field ordering.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.2 official Google research: Vertex AI generated-output control guidance and GenerationConfig documentation describe responseSchema, responseMimeType, required fields, propertyOrdering, and the warning against duplicating schema definitions in prompt text.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should adopt explicit plan-then-execute-then-narrate sequencing and ban past-tense completion wording before evidence exists. Later runtime tests should verify that the staged contract survives first-turn and carry-forward sessions.
fields.consult_when: Consult when editing the parent prompt, output contract, or agent instruction topology for truthful search and action narration.
fields.finding_summary: The strongest documented prompt-control pattern for truthful search and action narration is state-gated staged execution: decide whether grounding or a tool is needed, execute it, then narrate only from the returned evidence. Structured status fields can help, but they are not proof of execution unless validated against real results.
fields.source_basis: Official Google prompt-design, structured-output, and function-calling documentation reviewed in the 2026-04-22 T3.9.1 batch.

fields.design_impact: Later Gemini prompt-source and validation logic should anchor action-state semantics to StreamAssist-style progress, result, and terminal-state distinctions rather than to older legacy answer-path assumptions.
fields.finding_summary: For current Gemini Enterprise assistant behavior, Google recommends StreamAssist over the legacy Search-and-Answer path, and StreamAssist is the clearest documented surface for separating progress updates, tool-result linkage, terminal answer state, and final answer behavior.
fields.source_basis: Added during post-report completeness review from prompt_3 deep-research report, which explicitly called out StreamAssist as the recommended current surface and contrasted it with the older Search-and-Answer path.
fields.consult_when: Consult when defining truthful search/action state wording, runtime receipts, or which Google semantic surface should anchor current Gemini Enterprise action-boundary assumptions.

fields.design_impact: Enterprise web session behavior should be treated as weaker documentation evidence and stronger validation burden.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming enterprise web/no-code agents preserve context, session continuity, or remembered constraints the same way as Agent Engine.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise documents Answer API follow-up sessions and a separate personalization/memory layer, but generic enterprise web/no-code agent session semantics are much less explicit than Agent Engine session semantics.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 sessions and memory sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should prefer state-labeled progress text, bounded-unknown language, and result-linked wording; validation rows should fail on fake completion narration; runtime-only tests should confirm the visible transition from requested or in-progress to executed and returned-result states.
fields.consult_when: Consult before editing agent instructions, output-contract rules, or runtime-only test plans that target fake action narration or misleading search/tool chatter.
fields.finding_summary: Google-documented anti-fabrication patterns favor small explicit tool sets, structured outputs, clarifying questions instead of assumptions, low-variance generation, and progress wording that is visibly provisional rather than fake 'I'm doing X now' completion narration.
fields.source_basis: 2026-04-22 deep research report 'Gemini Enterprise Action Boundary Research' grounded in Google function-calling guidance and design recommendations for tool selection, schemas, clarifying questions, and low-hallucination settings.

fields.design_impact: Governed Gemini files should explicitly allow responses such as not connected, not verified from configured sources, or insufficient grounded support, and validation should fail fabricated groundedness when support metadata is absent.
fields.consult_when: Consult before revising fallback behavior, unavailable or unknown wording, or answer-confidence handling for enterprise-integrated Gemini answers.
fields.finding_summary: Not connected, not grounded, low-relevance, and no-grounding-metadata are documented possible outcomes; the assistant should treat them as first-class bounded states instead of silently completing with unsupported enterprise claims.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.4 bounded deep-research report in the 2026-04-22 session, based on official Google documentation for grounding metadata, support scores, claim-level grounding checks, and answer/assist skip behavior.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source and validation work should not collapse all public-web grounding into one undifferentiated capability. The environment can truthfully say it uses enterprise web grounding without implying the full breadth of standard Google Search grounding.
fields.finding_summary: Web Grounding for Enterprise is not identical to standard Google Search grounding. Google documents it as a more compliance-focused subset of web grounding, while standard Google Search grounding is broader and more up to date when those extra controls are not required.
fields.source_basis: Added during post-report completeness review from prompt_4 deep-research report, which documented Web Grounding for Enterprise as a curated, compliance-focused subset and standard Google Search grounding as the broader fresher option when compliance controls are not needed.
fields.consult_when: Consult when describing the current environment's web-grounding lane, interpreting enterprise-web-grounding behavior, or deciding whether prompt/source text should distinguish compliance-focused web grounding from broader public-web grounding.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source should name connected source families explicitly and forbid generic claims of enterprise-system access when only public web grounding or uploaded files are available.
fields.consult_when: Consult before writing or revising governed Gemini behavior for what the app can know, search, or verify from enterprise-integrated sources.
fields.finding_summary: Truthful enterprise-grounded answers must be scoped to the actually configured grounding path: enterprise data stores or Vertex grounding, Google Search or Web Grounding for Enterprise, a custom search API, or session-scoped uploaded context files.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.4 bounded deep-research report in the 2026-04-22 session, based on official Google documentation for enterprise data stores, Google Search grounding, Web Grounding for Enterprise, grounding with your search API, and addContextFile.

fields.design_impact: DCOIR should treat Agent Engine as the authoritative execution layer for runtime truthfulness and execution proof.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming Gemini Enterprise itself executes surfaced ADK-agent logic.
fields.finding_summary: When an ADK agent is registered into Gemini Enterprise, Gemini Enterprise serves as the discovery, sharing, and web-app access layer, while Vertex AI Agent Engine remains the runtime that processes the agent queries.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report on registered ADK agents surfaced in Gemini Enterprise. See attached report findings.

fields.design_impact: This is the strongest documented state-management and execution-trace surface for DCOIR runtime truthfulness.
fields.consult_when: Consult before making claims about session persistence, action traceability, or multi-turn runtime state on Vertex-side agents.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Engine Sessions are explicitly documented as the source of conversation context, and events can include actions such as function calls; ADK distinguishes local in-memory sessions from cloud-managed deployed sessions.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 sessions/tool execution sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: DCOIR validation must explicitly test observability completeness, region scope, and preview-surface reliability before closeout claims.
fields.consult_when: Consult before treating any runtime-evidence or monitoring surface as complete, stable, or globally available.
fields.finding_summary: Some key observability and runtime surfaces remain preview, bounded, or incomplete: Gemini Enterprise observability is preview, Agent Engine trace format is experimental, Example Store and agent identity are preview, Threat Detection is preview, Cloud Logging omits some subresources, and Code Execution is region-bounded.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 observability and must-test sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini work should treat assistant-config controls as real but insufficient proof of exact behavior. High-discipline response-contract claims must still be runtime-validated.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming that assistant-side controls are enough to enforce exact startup choreography, strict response contracts, or deterministic fallback behavior.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise assistant configuration documents additional LLM system instructions, web grounding choice, default web search state, location context, Model Armor, and banned phrases, but it does not document a deeply exposed deterministic runtime contract for planner behavior or response obedience.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 5-8 and source appendix page 12. Assistant configuration and control surfaces documented; deterministic planner/obedience contract not documented.

fields.design_impact: Later Gemini file updates should preserve executor type, invocation or tool IDs, terminal state, and result-linked evidence wherever the runtime can expose them, and should treat planner or status text as nonterminal by default.
fields.consult_when: Consult before defining runtime receipts, validation evidence, or post-action answer wording for searches, tool use, or delegated actions.
fields.finding_summary: Google surfaces progress and observability separately from completion: StreamAssist exposes status updates, terminal answer state, and final-result tool linkage, while ADK distinguishes tool-call requests, tool results, invocation IDs, and final responses.
fields.source_basis: 2026-04-22 deep research report 'Gemini Enterprise Action Boundary Research' grounded in Google docs for StreamAssist and ADK event and tool-result handling.

fields.design_impact: Grounding source selection is documented and important, but exact planner behavior and automatic use remain opaque.
fields.consult_when: Consult when scoping grounded search behavior and when testing whether grounding source choice affects response quality or evidence scope.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise assistant configuration documents Google Search or Enterprise web search grounding selection, default web-search state, and query-level override behavior.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 grounding/citation section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should preserve evidence-bearing citations and support phrasing. Validation rows should fail if Gemini claims grounded search completion without visible sources or equivalent support evidence in the returned answer path.
fields.consult_when: Consult when shaping citation language, source rendering, or final-answer wording for grounded Gemini replies.
fields.finding_summary: Grounded answers are evidence-bearing outputs, not plain narrative. Official docs describe grounding metadata, grounding chunks, grounding supports, and user-visible source rendering. Later governed Gemini source changes should preserve returned source objects and support mapping instead of paraphrasing groundedness away.
fields.source_basis: Official Google and Google Cloud grounding and Gemini Enterprise assistant-chat documentation reviewed in the 2026-04-22 T3.9.1 batch.

fields.design_impact: Identity and delegated authority are real design surfaces and should be treated as code-path/runtime concerns, not generic UI assumptions.
fields.consult_when: Consult before scoping least-privilege design, user-context propagation, or personalized/runtime identity behavior.
fields.finding_summary: Registered ADK agents surfaced through Gemini Enterprise can receive user email and OAuth authorization resources, while Agent Engine agent identity provides per-agent principal identities and delegated OAuth support.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 identity propagation section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: This is now the default design recommendation unless direct target-surface testing proves that the enterprise web/no-code path can safely carry stronger response-contract and execution-evidence obligations.
fields.consult_when: Consult before deciding how to anchor future Gemini agent design assumptions, test harnesses, and runtime-proof claims.
fields.finding_summary: The strongest documented DCOIR-style architecture remains split: Gemini Enterprise for enterprise discovery, connectors, permission-aware retrieval, and distribution; ADK plus Agent Engine plus Vertex grounding and evaluation for strict tooling, observability, memory discipline, and validation.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 executive summary, design implications, and builder-facing conclusions: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Custom operation availability through the Gemini Enterprise surface must be directly tested before being treated as supported.
fields.consult_when: Consult before designing surfaced ADK agents that depend on custom registered operations.
fields.finding_summary: Google does not document whether the Gemini Enterprise web app can invoke custom Agent Engine operations beyond the default conversational query and stream_query path for surfaced ADK agents.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report registration and control boundary section.

fields.design_impact: DCOIR should anchor any hard response-contract claims on Vertex/API execution paths, not on Gemini Enterprise web/no-code surfaces.
fields.consult_when: Consult before promising exact schema output, exact response envelopes, or machine-validated output shape.
fields.finding_summary: Vertex AI structured output is the clearest documented schema-constrained response contract in the stack, but it is API-defined, has supported-schema limits, and has no console support.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 response-contract section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini validation should separately test assistant-side behavior and user-created agent behavior instead of treating them as one instruction surface.
fields.consult_when: Consult before mixing assistant instruction evidence with Agent Designer instruction evidence or assuming that one surface proves the behavior of the other.
fields.finding_summary: The documentation distinguishes admin-created or admin-governed assistant surfaces from user-created no-code agents enabled through Agent Designer. Those are related but non-identical control surfaces.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 5-6. User-created Agent Designer agents and admin-created assistant behavior were described as distinct but related surfaces.

fields.design_impact: Routing can be influenced by description quality, but invocation remains probabilistic or opaque rather than a hard contract.
fields.consult_when: Consult before relying on agent description as a deterministic routing rule.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise registration stores display metadata including description, and the description is used by an LLM to decide whether to invoke the agent for a user query.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report registration and control boundary section.

fields.design_impact: Runtime-local confirmation and action gating should be implemented explicitly for DCOIR instead of assumed from the enterprise UI.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming the Gemini Enterprise UI will confirm or gate high-consequence actions performed by surfaced ADK agents.
fields.finding_summary: Google does not document a generic user-confirmation model for actions executed by registered ADK agents surfaced in Gemini Enterprise, unlike the explicit approval flows documented for native assistant actions.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report executed-vs-suggested section.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should explicitly separate requested action, proposed tool call, in-progress execution, and completed action with result. Validation rows should fail on state collapse or fake handoff narration that skips the executed-result boundary.
fields.consult_when: Consult when defining truthful action language, handoff wording, or tool-observability behavior.
fields.finding_summary: The model does not directly invoke tools or functions. It proposes structured function-call output and the application executes the tool, then returns the result. Completion claims are only truthful after a real execution result or action-state receipt exists.
fields.source_basis: Official Google and Google Cloud function-calling and Gemini Enterprise assistant-action documentation reviewed in the 2026-04-22 T3.9.1 batch.

fields.design_impact: Supports issue #44 follow-on work by confirming that dense instruction authoring is a documented surface, while still leaving obedience and parity to validation rather than assumption.
fields.consult_when: Consult when evaluating what no-code Gemini Enterprise agents can be configured to express and where instruction density can plausibly live in the enterprise web surface.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Designer is a documented no-code/low-code Gemini Enterprise builder surface with instructions, model selection, tools/data, knowledge uploads, starter prompts, preview/testing, flows, subagents, and schedules.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 5-8 and source appendix pages 12 and 15. Agent Designer creation and overview docs cited by the report.

fields.design_impact: Identity semantics are strong but not fully mature; production assumptions should remain bounded.
fields.consult_when: Consult before anchoring production-grade dual-principal proofs on Agent Engine agent identity.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Engine supports multiple authentication modes including agent identity, service accounts, API keys, and OAuth client IDs; agent identity can log both user identity and agent identity in delegated flows but remains preview and is recommended only for test environments.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report identity/authorization sections.

fields.design_impact: Enterprise app observability is useful but not a substitute for Agent Engine runtime evidence.
fields.consult_when: Consult before treating Gemini Enterprise observability as sufficient for execution proof or runtime-closeout claims.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise provides app-level observability for surfaced assistant and agent interactions, including traces, spans, metrics, and prompt/response logging, but these surfaces are Preview and represent the front-door request layer rather than full runtime proof.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report observability table.

fields.design_impact: Supports evidence-first DCOIR design by grounding future retrieval claims in the documented enterprise surface instead of generic model behavior assumptions.
fields.consult_when: Consult when designing grounded enterprise retrieval behavior, answer/citation expectations, or enterprise knowledge-source assumptions for the DCOIR Gemini agent.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise explicitly documents connectors, data stores, permission-aware enterprise retrieval, assistants, actions, agents, and file/context surfaces as part of the enterprise platform.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 5-6 and source appendix pages 11-13. Gemini Enterprise concepts, connectors, access control, and assistant/file chat behavior were cited in the report.

fields.design_impact: Safety controls must be implemented and validated in the runtime code path, not assumed from enterprise registration.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming enterprise UI safety inheritance for a surfaced ADK runtime.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Model Armor does not automatically protect registered ADK agents; Model Armor must be configured inside the agent application code through the REST API.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report executive summary and safety section.

fields.design_impact: This becomes the default DCOIR design stance unless direct testing shows stronger enterprise-surface guarantees than Google currently documents.
fields.consult_when: Consult before making design or validation decisions for the registered-ADK-in-Gemini-Enterprise path.
fields.finding_summary: For surfaced ADK agents, the safest documented architecture is to treat Gemini Enterprise as the delivery, discovery, and coarse observability layer while treating Agent Engine as the authoritative execution, safety, logging, tracing, and action-proof layer.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report design implications and conclusions.

fields.design_impact: Supports the DCOIR need for query broadening and search-truthfulness discipline. Retrieval misses should often be treated as test-required behavior rather than platform proof.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming that a narrow-query miss proves logs are absent, before overfitting retrieval behavior to one session, or before assuming stable ranking/planning from docs alone.
fields.finding_summary: Grounding and retrieval are first-class documented surfaces, but retrieval remains only partially configurable. Connectors, ACLs, filters, boosts, web grounding modes, and evaluations exist, while exact ranking and retrieval planning remain opaque and can drift over time.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 8-10 and source appendix pages 15-16. Retrieval configurability, evaluation, and periodic search-behavior change were explicitly discussed.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should require source-lane honesty: public-web grounding, enterprise-data grounding, uploaded-file reasoning, and no-live-connector state must be narrated distinctly instead of collapsed into one generic search claim.
fields.consult_when: Consult when deciding whether Gemini may truthfully say it searched the public web, searched enterprise data, or only used uploaded artifacts.
fields.finding_summary: Public web grounding and enterprise or your-data grounding are distinct documented paths. Google Search grounding is the public-web path, while Vertex AI Search grounding is the enterprise or your-data path. Later governed Gemini source changes should state that public citations do not prove enterprise connector search occurred and uploaded-file answers are a separate evidence lane.
fields.source_basis: Official Google and Google Cloud documentation reviewed in the 2026-04-22 T3.9.1 research batch.

fields.design_impact: This is the strongest documented Google layer for explicit runtime validation. Future Gemini behavior claims that need sessions, memory, evaluation, or observability should be mapped here first.
fields.consult_when: Consult before deciding where DCOIR Gemini runtime truth should be validated, where sessions/memory assumptions belong, and where evaluation/observability claims can be strongest.
fields.finding_summary: ADK is documented as an open-source, model-agnostic, deployment-agnostic framework, while Agent Engine is the managed runtime layer with sessions, memory, evaluation, code execution, and observability surfaces.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 6-9 and source appendix page 13. Agent Builder, ADK, Agent Engine, Sessions, and Memory Bank documentation were cited.

fields.design_impact: Execution proof requires runtime-side and downstream evidence, not just enterprise-surface telemetry.
fields.consult_when: Consult before using Gemini Enterprise UI or app-level artifacts alone as execution receipts.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise can expose coarse tool evidence such as invocationTools, tool count, and tool latency, but the docs do not support treating those artifacts as full proof of downstream execution for surfaced ADK agents.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report observability and must-test sections.

fields.design_impact: Cross-project registration is an IAM-governed runtime path, not just a UI configuration step.
fields.consult_when: Consult before setting up cross-project surfaced ADK registration or debugging cross-project access failures.
fields.finding_summary: Cross-project surfaced ADK use requires granting the Gemini Enterprise service agent in the app project the Discovery Engine Service Agent role in the agent project.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report identity and cross-project sections.

fields.design_impact: This supports evidence discipline and search-truthfulness evaluation, especially for API-side designs, but not universal web-surface fidelity assumptions.
fields.consult_when: Consult before designing evidence-first answer logic or retrieval-quality evaluation criteria.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Answer API documents citations, support scores, aggregated support score, support-threshold filtering, personalization inputs, filters, boosts, query classification, follow-up sessions, and rephrasing controls.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 grounding/citation and retrieval sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: This is the strongest documented Gemini Enterprise-side behavior-control surface for programmatic answer shaping, but it is still not a universal cross-surface system contract.
fields.consult_when: Consult when designing Answer API-side behavior shaping, evidence handling, and retrieval compensation behavior.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Answer API exposes preambles, citations, model selection, query rephrasing, query classification, follow-up sessions, and grounding-quality controls.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 executive summary and response-contract / retrieval sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Schema-first recommendations should stay compact and operational. Later governed changes should prefer a smaller enforceable schema over a large expressive schema that increases malformed-output or error risk.
fields.finding_summary: Over-complex structured-output schemas can reduce reliability or even fail outright. Google recommends shortening property names, flattening overly nested structures, reducing optional properties and enum bloat, and keeping the schema simpler when strict output compliance matters.
fields.source_basis: Added during post-report completeness review from prompt_2 deep-research report, which explicitly preserved Google guidance that over-complex response schemas can degrade reliability and trigger errors.
fields.consult_when: Consult when converting Gemini output contracts into schema-first structures or when tightening exact final-form output without making the contract brittle.

fields.design_impact: Governed Gemini wording should preserve states like available, awaiting authorization, awaiting confirmation, executing, succeeded, failed, and skipped instead of collapsing them into instant success claims.
fields.consult_when: Consult before adding or revising enterprise-integration behavior rules, especially any wording that implies email, calendar, or connected-app actions are already complete.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise native assistant actions are not unconditional completions. Google documents admin-enabled tools, user authorization of the connected service, and confirmation flows before actions such as creating calendar events or sending email.
fields.source_basis: 2026-04-22 deep research report 'Gemini Enterprise Action Boundary Research' grounded in Google docs for Gemini Enterprise assistant actions and configuration.

fields.design_impact: Provides the default architecture split for future DCOIR Gemini work until later research or live validation proves a stronger enterprise-web-app contract.
fields.consult_when: Consult before choosing where to anchor future DCOIR Gemini design assumptions, evaluation plans, and runtime-proof claims.
fields.finding_summary: For dense, analyst-facing, evidence-first enterprise agents, the most defensible documented pattern is to use Gemini Enterprise for enterprise distribution, connectors, access control, and grounded enterprise search, while relying on Vertex-side ADK and Agent Engine for stronger control over sessions, memory, runtime behavior, evaluation, observability, and code-path governance.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report executive summary and pages 9-10. This was the report's strongest builder-facing conclusion rather than a raw product claim.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini fixes involving startup flow, context carryover, or remembered constraints should distinguish enterprise web behavior from Agent Engine-documented runtime state.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming that enterprise web-app agents preserve session choreography, memory behavior, or cross-turn state the same way Agent Engine docs describe.
fields.finding_summary: Multi-turn state, sessions, and memory are explicitly documented on the Agent Engine and ADK side, while the enterprise web-app side is less explicit about session semantics for custom no-code agents.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 6-9. Sessions and Memory Bank are explicit on Agent Engine; enterprise web custom-agent session semantics remain less explicit.

fields.design_impact: Any strict no-code response-contract expectation remains validation-required and should not be promoted as documented fact.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming a Gemini Enterprise web/no-code agent will always answer in an exact shape.
fields.finding_summary: The reviewed Gemini Enterprise web-agent and Agent Designer docs do not document schema-constrained output, response validators, or exact response-envelope enforcement equivalent to Vertex structured output.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 response-contract section and must-test checklist: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: The strongest builder-facing evidence schema is on the Vertex/API side, making it the safest anchor for source-to-claim mapping requirements.
fields.consult_when: Consult when DCOIR needs claim-to-source traceability or evidence-first answer validation.
fields.finding_summary: Vertex grounding docs expose grounding metadata, chunks, support mappings for inline citations, and attribution/display expectations for Google Search and Vertex AI Search grounding.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 grounding/citation section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: This is important for separating prompt-source fixes from environment-remedy recommendations. Some desired enterprise knowledge behaviors cannot be achieved by wording alone if the retrieval path is still upload-only.
fields.finding_summary: When uploaded files are the only internal-knowledge path, the sanctioned ways to expand beyond upload-only behavior are to ingest enterprise content into first-party data stores, build custom connectors, or bridge an existing enterprise search system through search-API grounding. Uploads alone are not a substitute for organization-wide enterprise retrieval.
fields.source_basis: Added during post-report completeness review from prompt_4 deep-research report, which documented first-party data-store ingestion, custom connectors, and grounding with your search API as sanctioned enterprise knowledge-expansion paths when connectors are absent.
fields.consult_when: Consult when recommending future environment improvements, explaining why upload-only behavior is narrow, or deciding whether a desired enterprise knowledge behavior requires platform/configuration changes rather than prompt changes alone.

fields.design_impact: Identity propagation exists, but implementation details are still a must-test area.
fields.consult_when: Consult before making strong claims about identity-field mapping or stable personalization plumbing.
fields.finding_summary: Registered ADK agents receive the user's email address from Gemini Enterprise, but Google does not document the exact field or callback object by which that email is delivered into ADK code.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report identity boundary section.

fields.design_impact: Enterprise web observability should be treated as useful but bounded; DCOIR should not rely on it alone for strong runtime-proof claims.
fields.consult_when: Consult before treating Gemini Enterprise observability as complete, production-stable, or closeout-sufficient by itself.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise observability exists but is explicitly Preview and can include prompt and response logging with sensitive-data implications; it is not equivalent to the depth of Agent Engine runtime instrumentation.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 observability section and must-test list: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: This primarily affects prompt-source files. Cleaner prompt topology is a documented way to reduce repeated sections and scaffold-looking fragments without relying on downstream cleanup or post-processing.
fields.consult_when: Consult when final outputs show repeated BLUF or analysis blocks, duplicated section headings, mid-stream restarts, or prompt collisions between examples and output rules.
fields.finding_summary: Suppress duplicate drafts and noisy intermediate structure by removing redundant or conflicting instructions, separating instructions, context, and examples with delimiters, keeping few-shot examples minimal and consistently formatted, and breaking multi-step work into sub-prompts instead of stacking many tasks into one visible answer pass.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.2 official Google research: prompt-design, structure-prompts, break-down-prompts, and few-shot guidance all point toward delimiter separation, fewer conflicting instructions, and smaller well-aligned example sets.

fields.design_impact: Issue #44 and future instruction-density work must treat precedence as a live validation question, not a solved documentation fact.
fields.consult_when: Consult before declaring exact instruction precedence, enforcing strict response contracts from docs alone, or judging failures as pure instruction defects without runtime evidence.
fields.finding_summary: Public documentation does not state a single authoritative precedence hierarchy for system instructions, developer instructions, agent instructions, user messages, tools, and memory across all Gemini Enterprise and Vertex surfaces.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 7-10. Missing universal precedence model was explicitly called out as a documentation gap and test-required area.

fields.design_impact: Instruction-density and runtime-truthfulness defects must be treated as validation issues, not assumed documentation failures or assumed precedence truths.
fields.consult_when: Consult before making claims about exact instruction precedence, startup order, or deterministic obedience across mixed Gemini Enterprise and Vertex surfaces.
fields.finding_summary: Google documents multiple control surfaces, but does not publish a universal precedence hierarchy across assistant instructions, Agent Designer instructions, Answer API preambles, ADK instructions, memory, tools, and user prompts.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 executive summary and instruction/control hierarchy matrix. Attached report and research output: see turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Prevents future Gemini work from importing Workspace-native assumptions into the DCOIR enterprise-agent design line without explicit validation.
fields.consult_when: Consult before using Workspace documentation as proof for Gemini Enterprise behavior or assuming Workspace agent controls map directly onto Gemini Enterprise controls.
fields.finding_summary: Workspace with Gemini, the Gemini app, and Workspace Studio are adjacent enterprise AI surfaces, not interchangeable names for Gemini Enterprise.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 1-6 and source appendix pages 14-16. Workspace Studio and Workspace admin docs are documented as separate surfaces from Gemini Enterprise console controls.

fields.design_impact: User-authorized access paths must be explicitly configured and validated as part of the registration/runtime design.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming user-delegated resource access is automatic for a surfaced ADK agent.
fields.finding_summary: Delegated Google Cloud access for surfaced ADK agents is not implicit; Gemini Enterprise registration supports an explicit authorization resource attachment through authorizationConfig.toolAuthorizations.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report registration and authorization sections.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini fixes should treat enterprise web-app behavior and Vertex runtime behavior as related but non-identical evidence surfaces. Runtime guarantees should not be claimed from enterprise integration alone.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming what the platform can do natively, deciding where validation should occur, or mapping custom-agent behavior into the enterprise web surface.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise is the enterprise distribution/search/assistant/agent surface, while Vertex AI Agent Builder, ADK, and Agent Engine are the builder/runtime/evaluation stack. Integration does not erase that boundary.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report executive summary and pages 1-9. Core platform split documented across Gemini Enterprise, Agent Builder, ADK, and Agent Engine references.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source should separate preliminary progress from finished work, validation should fail on collapsed states, and runtime tests should verify that wording changes as the state advances.
fields.consult_when: Consult before editing prime-agent, parent, or output-contract rules that explain what the assistant is doing versus what has finished.
fields.finding_summary: The safest truthful state model is four-layer: planned action, requested action, executed action, and returned result. Only the executed-action layer should authorize user-facing completion claims such as 'done', 'sent', 'searched', or 'retrieved'.
fields.source_basis: 2026-04-22 deep research report 'Gemini Enterprise Action Boundary Research' synthesized into a governance-ready action-state model for later Gemini source updates.

fields.design_impact: User-facing evidence and citation behavior remain must-test areas for the registered-ADK integration path.
fields.consult_when: Consult before claiming the user-facing Gemini Enterprise surface provides complete evidence or citation fidelity for a surfaced ADK agent.
fields.finding_summary: Google does not document durable execution receipts, tool-by-tool transcripts, function-call argument visibility, or surfaced citation/source-attribution behavior specific to registered ADK-agent answers in the Gemini Enterprise web UI.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report user-facing evidence and must-test sections.

fields.design_impact: Prevents future Gemini work from collapsing legacy Agentspace naming into the current product identity or mislabeling the enterprise surface in governed DCOIR outputs.
fields.consult_when: Consult before describing the product surface, writing instructions that name the platform, or mapping enterprise features to builder/runtime layers.
fields.finding_summary: Use Gemini Enterprise as the current enterprise product name and treat Agentspace as a legacy or residual naming layer unless the task is specifically about historical naming, IAM/API leftovers, or documentation ambiguity.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 'Current Google Gemini Enterprise Agent Ecosystem' pages 1-5 and source appendix pages 11-16. Product naming boundary documented as Gemini Enterprise primary with residual Agentspace terminology.

fields.design_impact: Prevents future Gemini work from overstating product capabilities or admin controls when the documentation is edition-split.
fields.consult_when: Consult before using one edition's documentation as proof for another edition or assuming feature/admin parity across business and enterprise documentation splits.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Business edition is documented separately from Standard and Plus, so behaviors and admin controls cannot be casually inferred across editions.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report pages 2-4 and page 10. Edition split and separate documentation channels were explicitly called out.

fields.design_impact: If DCOIR needs to prove what was executed versus suggested, Agent Engine is the most defensible documented surface.
fields.consult_when: Consult before deciding where DCOIR runtime proof, execution evidence, or observability-closeout standards should anchor.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Engine provides the strongest documented execution evidence surfaces: session events with function calls, tracing, logging, monitoring, and code execution boundaries.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 tool execution and observability sections: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Supports dense instruction authoring as a documented capability, while leaving obedience and response-contract enforcement to testing.
fields.consult_when: Consult before judging what the no-code enterprise builder surface can express and before scoping instruction-density fixes.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Agent Designer explicitly documents instructions, model choice, data/tools, knowledge upload, starter prompts, subagents, schedules, and preview/testing.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 matrix and source appendix: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: This belongs in prompt-source and validation: answer exact build or provenance only from exposed governed or product metadata; otherwise return bounded unknown plus sanctioned maintenance path.
fields.consult_when: Consult before editing version or build answer behavior and before validating citation or provenance-style receipts in enterprise-integrated Gemini outputs.
fields.finding_summary: Version, build, and provenance answers must be limited to metadata the app actually exposes. If only aliases such as stable or preview, or visible grounding metadata, are available, the app should say so and avoid invented build numbers, hidden rollout details, or hidden provenance narratives.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.4 bounded deep-research report in the 2026-04-22 session, based on official Google documentation for model aliases, grounding display requirements, and grounding or provenance metadata exposure.

fields.design_impact: Session mapping across enterprise UI and runtime remains a direct-validation requirement.
fields.consult_when: Consult before assuming session continuity, replay, or traceability across the surfaced ADK integration path.
fields.finding_summary: Google does not document whether a surfaced ADK conversation in Gemini Enterprise creates only Gemini Enterprise sessions, only Agent Engine sessions, or both, nor how those session identifiers relate.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report registration/control and must-test sections.

fields.design_impact: Prompt-source files should request only the final operator-facing artifact unless explicit reasoning output is truly required. Internal planning should not be stored in a form whose accidental exposure would break the operator contract.
fields.consult_when: Consult when prime-agent or sub-agent instructions currently request step-by-step reasoning, expose planning text, or rely on hidden scaffold that must never become operator-visible.
fields.finding_summary: Do not ask for visible reasoning in final-form operator output. Keep includeThoughts off; for supported Flash paths use minimal or zero thinking budget when quality allows. Do not treat system instructions as a secure hidden-scaffold store because Google says system instructions guide behavior but do not fully prevent leaks or jailbreak exposure.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.2 official Google research: explain-reasoning guidance, thinking controls docs, GenerationConfig thought controls, and system-instruction docs show that visible reasoning requests surface reasoning text and that system instructions are not a leak-proof hidden scaffold location.

fields.design_impact: The enterprise assistant path has meaningful safety and authorization boundaries, but that does not prove equivalent evidence or control for custom no-code or registered-agent paths.
fields.consult_when: Consult before describing enterprise assistant action safety boundaries or claiming silent execution behavior.
fields.finding_summary: Enterprise assistant actions are explicitly admin-dependent, user-authorized, and confirmation-oriented; users must authorize connected services before actions can run.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 tool use/actions section: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Query broadening and fallback behavior remain validation-required for DCOIR Gemini work.
fields.consult_when: Consult before concluding that a retrieval miss proves absence of evidence or before claiming platform-side auto-broadening as a feature guarantee.
fields.finding_summary: The docs support API-side rephrasing and fallback controls, but do not publish a universal guarantee that a narrow search miss will always be broadened, reformulated, or automatically rescued.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 retrieval failure / fallback section and must-test list: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Future DCOIR Gemini testing should explicitly validate region, maturity, and evaluation-scope assumptions instead of inheriting them from documentation headlines.
fields.consult_when: Consult before treating every documented runtime feature as uniformly production-ready or before designing validation gates that assume broad region/feature parity.
fields.finding_summary: Some important runtime and evaluation surfaces remain bounded or incomplete in public docs, including limits like code execution documented only for us-central1, preview/experimental surfaces, and evaluation constraints such as search-quality evaluation not covering multi-data-store apps.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report page 10. The report explicitly called out code execution region limits, preview surfaces, and evaluation constraints as unresolved or caution-worthy.

fields.design_impact: Memory safety remains a runtime governance problem and should be treated conservatively for DCOIR.
fields.consult_when: Consult before enabling long-term memory in a surfaced ADK analyst agent.
fields.finding_summary: If a surfaced ADK agent uses Agent Engine Memory Bank, memory-poisoning and prompt-injection risks remain runtime concerns, and Google does not document any extra mitigation automatically added by surfacing the agent in Gemini Enterprise.
fields.source_basis: Third deep-research report safety boundary and must-test sections.

fields.design_impact: Governed Gemini files should say uploaded files are the only current internal-knowledge path when true and should avoid implying organization-wide search; later runtime tests should distinguish upload-only behavior from connector-backed enterprise retrieval.
fields.consult_when: Consult before answering questions about internal knowledge coverage, uploads, or enterprise search availability in the current Gemini environment.
fields.finding_summary: When enterprise connectors or internal search are absent, uploaded files are a real but narrow internal-knowledge path: session-scoped or personal-content style access, not equivalent to organization-wide enterprise retrieval.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.4 bounded deep-research report in the 2026-04-22 session, based on official Google documentation for uploaded context files, data-store ingestion, custom connectors, and grounding with your search API.

fields.design_impact: This belongs in runtime or config-side generation settings rather than long prose. It reduces run-on output, trailing duplicate sections, and accidental multi-draft behavior without widening prompt complexity.
fields.consult_when: Consult when tuning operator-facing final answers, preventing repeated sections, or turning a free-form answer path into a one-answer contract.
fields.finding_summary: Use single-answer low-variance generation hygiene for operator-facing outputs: candidateCount 1, low temperature, greedy or tightly constrained decoding where supported, bounded maxOutputTokens, optional stopSequences, and fixed seed for reproducibility checks. Google documents this as mostly deterministic, not fully deterministic.
fields.source_basis: T3.9.2 official Google research: Vertex AI prompt-parameter guidance and GenerationConfig docs describe temperature, seed, topK, candidateCount, maxOutputTokens, and stopSequences for best-effort deterministic shaping.

fields.design_impact: Governed Gemini source should not narrate planned or requested actions as completed work. Completion language must be reserved for cases where an executed-action receipt or returned result is actually present.
fields.consult_when: Consult before writing any Gemini prompt-source or output-contract text that claims a lookup, tool use, delegated action, or search has already happened.
fields.finding_summary: Google-documented function calling and tool use draw a hard boundary between model intent and execution: planner text, status text, and function-call payloads are request artifacts, while the host app, extension runtime, Gemini assistant action layer, or downstream agent service performs the actual execution.
fields.source_basis: 2026-04-22 deep research report 'Gemini Enterprise Action Boundary Research' normalized from official Google and Google Cloud docs on Vertex AI function calling, Extensions, Gemini Enterprise StreamAssist, assistant actions, A2A, and ADK events.

fields.design_impact: Any DCOIR memory-enabled design needs explicit poisoning-risk handling and direct runtime validation.
fields.consult_when: Consult before enabling long-term memory assumptions or treating memory as low-risk state.
fields.finding_summary: Agent Engine Memory Bank is explicitly cross-session and user-scoped and Google explicitly warns about memory poisoning and prompt injection risks; Gemini Enterprise has a separate personalization/memory layer with less operationally explicit controls.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 memory section and must-test list: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Confirms assistant-level control is real, but only local to that surface and not a proof of universal control over downstream runtime behavior.
fields.consult_when: Consult before designing or validating assistant-level behavior shaping on the enterprise surface.
fields.finding_summary: Gemini Enterprise Plus assistant configuration explicitly exposes additional LLM system instructions, grounding mode, location context, Model Armor, banned phrases, and chat-retention settings.
fields.source_basis: Deep research report 2 control hierarchy matrix and executive summary: turn80file0.

fields.design_impact: Keep exact UI-proof behaviors in validation or runtime-only categories until the deployed Gemini Enterprise environment shows them directly. Do not over-promote doc-aligned but unobserved behaviors into unconditional runtime claims.
fields.consult_when: Consult when deciding which items stay as prompt-source edits versus validation rows versus later runtime-only tests.
fields.finding_summary: Enterprise web or no-code surfaces still require runtime testing for exact citation rendering, source highlighting, public-grounding receipts, and action-state honesty. Public docs define the evidence model and boundaries, but fresh-session runtime validation is still required before treating those behaviors as fully proven in the deployed environment.
fields.source_basis: Official Google and Google Cloud documentation reviewed in the 2026-04-22 T3.9.1 batch, with runtime proof still pending in the deployed enterprise environment.
